uses: Bahel schulli, also known as sweet flag or Acorus calamus, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's roots are commonly used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and gas. It is also believed to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. In addition, Bahel schulli is said to have a calming effect on the nervous system, making it useful for treating anxiety and insomnia. The plant's essential oils are also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
